text: ECC is a profiler and optimizer that sits under the agent harness.

evidence.excerpt: ECC: a profiler and optimizer that sits under the harness
Marketing entry point for ECC, the harness-native agent operating system; lists stars, sponsors, guides, and version history across harnesses.
Agent instruction doc: 67 agents, 271 skills, 92 commands, core principles, orchestration, security, testing, and workflow policy.

text: ECC instruments every agent hop — tool calls, model routes, token cost — with zero prompt changes.

evidence.excerpt: Instruments every agent hop — tool calls, model routes, token cost — with zero prompt changes.

text: ECC auto-routes each step to the cheapest model that still passes its eval.

evidence.excerpt: Auto-routes each step to the cheapest model that still passes its eval.
Provider/model chosen from explicit arg, then env vars, then saved .llm.env file, defaulting to claude.

text: A regression gate blocks any change that makes an agent slower or more expensive.

evidence.excerpt: Regression gate blocks any change that makes an agent slower or more expensive.

text: ECC drops in beside existing harnesses with no rewrite and no lock-in.

evidence.excerpt: Drops in beside existing harnesses; no rewrite, no lock-in.
Same repo ships across Codex, Claude Code, Cursor, OpenCode, Gemini, Zed, and Copilot harnesses.

text: ECC delivers sub-100ms agent routing in production.

evidence.excerpt: ECC delivers sub-100ms agent routing in production.
UserPromptSubmit runs on every single message - adds latency to every prompt.
In our 50-task benchmark, mgrep + Claude Code used ~2x fewer tokens than grep-based workflows — a token benchmark, not a sub-100ms routing-latency measurement.

text: A live dashboard traces, ranks, and cost-attributes every agent hop in real time.

evidence.excerpt: Every hop traced, ranked, and attributed to a cost in real time.

text: ECC is priced per traced agent-run.

evidence.excerpt: Sits next to the tracing tools teams already pay for, priced per traced agent-run.
Repo is MIT-licensed forever; ECC Pro GitHub App ($19/seat/mo) funds private-repo hosting.

text: A working version of ECC has shipped and is open-sourced.

evidence.excerpt: Working harness instrumentation shipped and open-sourced.
Repo is MIT-licensed forever; ECC Pro GitHub App ($19/seat/mo) funds private-repo hosting.
npm package manifest for ecc-universal v2.0.0; declares files shipped (agents, skills, hooks, rules, scripts, mcp-configs) and metadata.

text: 9 design partners are running ECC against their own agent stacks.

evidence.excerpt: 9 design partners running ECC against their own agent stacks.

text: A median 38% token-cost reduction was observed across partner workloads.

evidence.excerpt: Median 38% token-cost reduction observed across partner workloads.

text: ECC has 410 GitHub stars and 27 external contributors in the first 8 weeks.

evidence.excerpt: 410 GitHub stars, 27 external contributors in the first 8 weeks.
